verbGlossary.ADMINISTERS: {source} is responsible for managing and maintaining compliance with {target} on the project (e.g., safety manager administers OSHA hazard program, contract administrator manages AIA contract provisions)
verbGlossary.LOGS PER: {source} records project events using {target} required documentation format (e.g., safety officer logs incidents per OSHA 300-series recordkeeping rules)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES UNDER: {source} assigns records to categories defined by {target} taxonomy (e.g., safety manager classifies injury reports under ANSI injury types)
verbGlossary.INSPECTS AGAINST: {source} conducts field inspections using {target} acceptance criteria as the pass/fail benchmark (e.g., QC inspector inspects installations against specification section tolerances)
verbGlossary.ORGANIZES BY: {source} structures inspection checklists, punch lists, or quality plans using {target} divisions (e.g., quality manager organizes QC program by MasterFormat division)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS: {source} applies a classification code from {target} to project line items or records (e.g., project manager assigns standard cost codes to budget lines and change orders)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES BY: {source} builds the project breakdown hierarchy using {target} system (e.g., project manager structures cost and schedule by WBS codes)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATES TO: {source} distributes project costs or budgets into {target} line structure (e.g., cost engineer allocates contingency funds to budget code lines)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS TO: {source} compares project performance metrics against {target} published industry norms (e.g., cost engineer benchmarks project cost performance to AACE standards)
verbGlossary.ESTIMATES BY: {source} develops cost estimates organized according to {target} work breakdown (e.g., estimator builds takeoff and pricing by MasterFormat divisions)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES BY: {source} measures building elements using {target} assembly-level structure (e.g., estimator quantifies systems and assemblies by UniFormat categories)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} ensures field crews comply with {target} requirements on the jobsite (e.g., superintendent enforces OSHA hazard controls and NFPA fire safety during active construction)
verbGlossary.VERIFIES AGAINST: {source} confirms installed work meets {target} requirements through field observation or testing (e.g., field engineer verifies concrete placement against specification section requirements)
verbGlossary.MODELS UNDER: {source} authors or coordinates digital models classified according to {target} element taxonomy (e.g., BIM manager models building components under IFC classification)
verbGlossary.TAGS TO: {source} applies {target} codes to digital model objects or coordination records for downstream filtering (e.g., BIM manager tags model elements to OmniClass table entries)
verbGlossary.SELECTS: {source} chooses the applicable category from {target} that governs project execution strategy (e.g., procurement manager selects delivery method classification for subcontract packages)
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS FOR: {source} performs functional testing and verification specifically to satisfy the target certification program's requirements (e.g., CxA commissions mechanical systems for LEED energy credits)
verbGlossary.TESTS PER: {source} executes test procedures prescribed by {target} (e.g., CxA tests building envelope per energy code standard protocols)
verbGlossary.BALANCES PER: {source} adjusts and certifies system performance using {target} prescribed procedures and tolerances (e.g., TAB technician balances HVAC airflow per NFPA ventilation standards)
verbGlossary.DESIGNS TO: {source} develops project design documents that comply with {target} code's prescriptive or performance requirements (e.g., design manager designs structural systems to IBC requirements)
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES BY: {source} builds and maintains the project schedule organized according to {target} work breakdown structure (e.g., scheduler builds activity network by WBS code hierarchy)
verbGlossary.CONTROLS PER: {source} manages document revisions, distributions, and approvals following {target} documentation requirements (e.g., document controller maintains submittal logs per specification section requirements)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} measure is broken down by {target} for comparative analysis (e.g., safety staffing ratio segmented by safety classification completeness across projects)
